title: Fixing CSS Grid Layout Bugs: Inconsistent Row Height Calculation

statement.markdown: Given a CSS grid layout with dynamic item heights, write a function to calculate the correct row height. The grid layout has a fixed number of rows and columns. Items within each row may have varying heights. The function should return the maximum row height. If two rows have the same maximum height, return the index of the first row.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an object containing the grid layout dimensions (rows, columns), the item heights, and a flag to indicate whether to include the grid line height in the calculation.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be the maximum row height or the index of the first row with the maximum height if two rows have the same maximum height.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input will always be valid and that the grid layout will not be empty.

constraints.description: Number of rows in the grid layout
Number of columns in the grid layout
Flag to indicate whether to include the grid line height in the calculation

hints.text: First hint text: Review the CSS Grid Layout specification to understand how row heights are calculated.
